items.title: Several infrastructure projects put on backburner as ACT 'at peak construction'

children.content: Some ACT government infrastructure projects, including health, emergency services and sports facilities in North Gungahlin, will be deferred to save about $700 million over four years.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a navy suit jacket and glasses sits indoors speaking with his hands.

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a navy suit jacket and glasses sits indoors speaking with his hands.

items.title: Even kids want gambling ads banned, so why won't the government do it?

children.content: The government's proposed crackdown on gambling ads doesn't go as far as most Australians want. When you run the numbers, it's a win for the betting industry.

image.altText: Hands holding a mobile phone displaying a gambling app

items.title: Knitters find key to serenity during 'party, party, party' snow season

children.content: As the NSW ski season gets underway, Jindabyne's Snowy Stitchers Social Club finds connection and mindfulness in needles and yarn.

image.altText: Rachelle Edwards, wearing a knitted beanie and gloves, standings in front of a snow field.

items.title: 'Cybertongue' could prevent 70 million tonnes of milk waste every year

children.content: About a sixth of the world's milk is wasted, over 150 million tonnes every year, but a Canberra company has come up with new technology that could drastically reduce that.

gallery.altText: A line of two-litre milk bottles in a processing facility.

thumbnail.altText: A line of two-litre milk bottles in a processing facility.

items.title: Canberra man facing almost 70 sexual assault, violence charges

children.content: Police said multiple women contacted them in late 2025 and early 2026 to accuse the man of sexual assault while he was in relationships with them.

image.altText: A man with his hands cuffed behind his back is led away by a police officer.

items.title: Brumbies set unwanted records as winless Aussie finals run continues

children.content: The ACT Brumbies have been dumped out of the Super Rugby Pacific finals series after setting unwanted club records in Wellington on Friday.

image.altText: Rugby players with hands on hips, looking dejected

items.title: Canberra Liberals say alleged violent threat was a 'misunderstanding'

children.content: The Canberra Liberals have distanced themselves from former leader Leanne Castley's allegation that she was threatened with physical assault, saying the remark related to an "animated discussion" that was quickly resolved.

gallery.altText: Two politicians speak at a press conference.

thumbnail.altText: Two politicians speak at a press conference.

items.title: PM pays tribute to 'personal friend' and former neighbour Richard Scolyer

children.content: Loved ones of former Australian of the Year Richard Scolyer have paid tribute to his legacy, with his family saying he "wanted cancer patients to know they weren't alone".

articles.title: What is glioblastoma and why is it so hard to treat?

children.content: Glioblastoma claims the lives of 200,000 people each year globally. Despite decades of research and funding, survival rates remain low.

image.altText: An x-ray type image showing a tumour inside a human brain.

articles.title: Richard Scolyer, who challenged the world to 'be brave, be bold', dies aged 59

children.content: The 2024 Australian of the Year Richard Scolyer has died. In his final message he urged all Australians to "pursue their dreams and passion with humility, love and compassion".

image.altText: Tight close-up poortrait of a man with light hair and eyebrows, smiling, wearing a blue-collared shirt. Greenery blurred in back

gallery.altText: Professor Richard Scolyer during the 2024 Australian of the Year Awards
Richard Scolyer with his wife Katie and three kids outdoor sofa

thumbnail.altText: Professor Richard Scolyer during the 2024 Australian of the Year Awards
Richard Scolyer with his wife Katie and three kids outdoor sofa
Professor Scolyer with Gail O'Brien and Anthony Albanese

items.title: UK, France and Germany back Zelenskyy's call for meeting with Putin

children.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has received the backing of the leaders of the UK, France and Germany in calling for direct ceasefire talks with Russia.

image.altText: Britain's Prime Minister Keir Starmer, France's President Emmanuel Macron, Ukrain'e President Volodymyr Zelenskyy pose for photo

items.title: Anthropic has its eyes on Australia and a large slice of its electricity output

children.content: The world's biggest tech titans see Australia as a promising investment opportunity for their AI ambitions, but some fear the federal government's regulatory retreat could lead to harm.

image.altText: A person holding an iPhone with Character.aI, ChatGPT, Claude and Grok chatbot apps on screen.

items.title: Undersea volcano inundates island with 'desert' of stone

children.content: Residents are concerned about access to food and fresh water as pumice stone makes travel difficult for Papua New Guinea's seafaring communities.

gallery.altText: Two people with sticks walk along a coastline with stone floating on the ocean behind them
Brown rocks obscure the ocean which laps at a shoreline lined with green trees
A cloud of white smoke steams up from under a deep blue ocean

thumbnail.altText: Two people with sticks walk along a coastline with stone floating on the ocean behind them
Brown rocks obscure the ocean which laps at a shoreline lined with green trees
A cloud of white smoke steams up from under a deep blue ocean

items.title: When a detective told a witness Joanna was pregnant, she took police to VCAT

children.content: A woman who says she suffered life-changing stress and anxiety — and later, the death of one of her babies — after a Victoria Police officer unlawfully disclosed that she was pregnant wants her story known.

image.altText: A woman with brunette hair poses for a photo in a leafy setting wearing a pink top and blue scarf

items.title: Top neurologists say regulator ignored warnings as weed killer decision looms

children.content: Australia's leading movement disorder specialists say the chemical regulator has not engaged with their concerns about paraquat, despite growing evidence linking the herbicide to Parkinson's disease.

image.altText: A man wearing dark jumper and beige puffer coat speaks, seen from behind the shoulder of someone wearing purple

items.title: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan decries sexist Melbourne billboard

children.content: Premier Jacinta Allan has been joined by Anthony Albanese, Julia Gillard and other politicians — except for Pauline Hanson — in denouncing the language used to describe her on political signs seen in the Melbourne CBD.

gallery.altText: A billboard saying Ditch the Witch

thumbnail.altText: A billboard saying Ditch the Witch

items.title: Indigenous families want 'crisis' investigated after 15 deaths in six months

children.content: Remote Indigenous communities across Australia are painfully aware that their life expectancy rates are worsening — and in one Northern Territory community, residents are calling for an investigation into a local death rate they feel is "not normal".

image.altText: Aboriginal senior woman and younger Aboriginal man sitting together, serious expression, in front of green leaves.

items.title: Ignoring One Nation like 'ignoring will of people', WA opposition leader warns

children.content: The WA Liberal leader says growing support for One Nation reflects frustration with Labor, and that he is open to finding a way of working with Pauline Hanson's party.

image.altText: Basil Zempilas stands at a podium and addresses the crowd

items.title: Secrets of Indigenous astronomy revealed in new exhibition

children.content: Newcastle Art Gallery presents the distinctive and intricate work of Torres Strait Islander artist Brian Robinson in its first major solo survey following a four-year refurbishment.

gallery.altText: A Torres Strait Islander man with a grey beard standing with his arms crossed in front of a large artwork.
Visitors in a gallery standing in front of a large black-and-white vinylcut print etched with images and patterns.
A black and white lino-cut adorned with colourful flower-like sculptural elements.

A black and white lino-cut adorned with colourful flower-like sculptural elements.

items.title: History quiz: When was the first Mars Exploration Rover launch?

children.content: What make of car was stolen in the world's first official vehicle theft? And do you know where the Endeavour was repaired after hitting the Great Barrier Reef in 1770?

image.altText: A black and white image of a robot arm surveying the rocky, barren surface of Mars.

items.title: With his pet chihuahua and trusty gopher, 97yo grazier won't slow down

children.content: At 97, Doug Harrison still scoots around his outback station, doing as much work as he can. As he nears a century of living, he reflects on memorable moments over the decades, with a cuppa in hand and his trusty chihuahua by his side.

image.altText: a man with black sunglasses holding the steering wheel of a vehicle with a white small dog sitting next to him

items.title: The electric classic car that now drives like a 'pocket rocket'

children.content: It may look like a small vintage car, but an EV conversion has brought it "up to speed" to its modern counterparts.

gallery.altText: David Dyer kneeling next to blue small classic car
Blue small car on the road

thumbnail.altText: David Dyer kneeling next to blue small classic car
Blue small car on the road

avatar.alt: Millie Roberts wearing a pink top and earrings and an 'M' necklace

items.title: SA government says crackdown is leading to illicit tobacco price increase

children.content: South Australia's Consumer and Business Affairs Minister says a price increase for illegal tobacco noticed by inspectors within stores shows the trade is being disrupted.

image.altText: Cigarettes and vapes on a table

items.title: Byron Bay park bashing leaves Canadian tourist with brain bleed

children.content: A 28-year-old Canadian tourist is in hospital with fractured skull after a brutal assault by strangers in a park at Byron Bay.

image.altText: A policer van parked outside a police station with a police sign on the background.

items.title: Suppression order extended for name of high-profile Qld man in extortion case

children.content: A magistrate has extended an order preventing the publication of details of the alleged extortion case, including the identities of the people involved.

image.altText: A woman in a pink blazer walking next to a man in a suit.

items.title: Family loses everything as large fire destroys house

children.content: Fallen powerlines electrified a metal fence encircling the home before a gas cylinder "went off like a rocket" as a house in Warilla is engulfed within minutes.

image.altText: image of house on fire

items.title: Does being in a 'safe seat' influence whether your hospital gets an upgrade?

children.content: Are hospitals in safe seats being overlooked for multi-million-dollar refurbishments by the Victorian government?

avatar.alt: A profile image of a young woman with long, brown hair.

items.title: Piastri promoted after penalty as teen sensation wins wild Monaco GP

children.content: Teenage sensation Kimi Antonelli was flawless to lead every lap of a drama-filled Monaco Grand Prix, claiming a fifth consecutive win to extend his lead in the Formula 1 drivers' championship. Follow live.

image.altText: Oscar Piastri at the Monaco Grand Prix 2026

items.title: Korda wins US Women's Open title with perilous final putt

children.content: Nelly Korda claims the US Women's Open for her second consecutive major victory, holding off Charley Hull and Gaby Lopez by one shot in Los Angeles.

image.altText: Nelly Korda holds the US Women's Open trophy.

items.title: Maths genius, devoted mum, headline-making CEO on King's Birthday Honours roll

children.content: Almost 950 Australians are honoured, including five stand-out recipients who are now Companions of the Order.

gallery.altText: A graphic with the images of three women and two men.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic with the images of three women and two men.

items.title: Perth actor wants police to pay after apartment 'destroyed' during murder probe

children.content: Troy Coward's investment property was the scene of a brutal murder involving his tenants in 2012. More than 13 years later, he is fighting for compensation from police over the impact he says their investigation had on his health and finances.

image.altText: Man with short hair and dark brown blazer, arms folded, with trees and pathway in background

items.title: How this Indigenous mother paved the way for her kids to study medicine

children.content: Ieesha, Abdul and Jamaal Nasser are following in the footsteps of their mother Tatum Maybir, who entered medicine aged 45 after raising her six children.

image.altText: A family of doctors

items.title: Outback cake decorator stops reporting vandalism because 'nothing gets done'

children.content: After three vandalism incidents in three years, cake decorator Ishani Harney says the emotional and financial toll of crime lingers long after the damage is repaired.

gallery.altText: Mount Isa cake decorator Ishani Harney holds a shard of glass from her shopfront window that was broken during a 2023 break-in.

thumbnail.altText: Mount Isa cake decorator Ishani Harney holds a shard of glass from her shopfront window that was broken during a 2023 break-in.

items.title: Crossbench MPs demand answers from Tasmanian premier, issue ultimatum

children.content: Seven crossbench MPs have written to Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff to demand answers about former minister Madeleine Ogilvie's resignation from the ministry, giving him until Friday morning to provide "direct and exact answers".

image.altText: Jeremy Rockliff and Madeleine Ogilvie in parliament.

items.title: Victorian teachers sue over alleged unpaid superannuation

children.content: A class action alleges the Education Department short-changed teachers some $100 million in superannuation contributions between 1995 and 2023.

image.altText: A woman with chin length wavy blonde hair with a two-toned orange scarf around her neck sits with patchworking on her lap.

items.title: Rare bird sighting excites twitchers

children.content: Birdwatchers from across Perth and even interstate have flocked to WA's Midwest after the sighting of a black-headed gull.

gallery.altText: a silver grey gull with black head wings spread in flight over a beach

thumbnail.altText: a silver grey gull with black head wings spread in flight over a beach
